strict_win_deltas.delta: P1 preserves the original single global `express.json()` call, only adding a `verify` callback to capture the raw body, while P2 removes that line, adds a second route-scoped `express.raw({type:'*/*'})` parser plus manual `JSON.parse`, and relocates the original `app.use(express.json())` to after the new `/webhook` route, a more invasive restructuring of the middleware chain.

P2's `express.raw({ type: '*/*' })` captures the raw body regardless of Content-Type, whereas P1's global `express.json()` only parses `application/json`-typed bodies, so a webhook delivered with any other content-type would leave `req.rawBody` undefined and make `crypto.createHmac(...).update(req.rawBody)` throw instead of returning a controlled response.

P1's shape check uses only `typeof req.body.timestamp !== 'number'`, matching the spec's literal 'timestamp is a number' wording, while P2 additionally requires `Number.isFinite(body.timestamp)`, rejecting NaN/Infinity timestamps that the spec text alone would still treat as valid.

P2 extracts shape validation into a standalone named `isValidWebhookBody` function called once, whereas P1 inlines the whole multi-condition shape check directly in the route handler and additionally re-invokes `hasValidWebhookSignature` from a second, generic-looking `app.use((err, req, res, next) => ...)` error middleware that special-cases `req.path === '/webhook'`.
